result.output:
Axial contrast-enhanced CT image of the upper abdomen.

Shows the liver, stomach, spleen, pancreas region, aorta, and upper abdominal soft tissues. There is a large, well-circumscribed low-attenuation cystic-appearing lesion in the left upper abdomen, adjacent to the stomach/pancreatic region, causing mass effect on nearby structures. No obvious calcification or gas is visible within it on this image.

modalities.text:
Axial contrast-enhanced CT image of the upper abdomen.

Shows the liver, stomach, spleen, pancreas region, aorta, and upper abdominal soft tissues. There is a large, well-circumscribed low-attenuation cystic-appearing lesion in the left upper abdomen, adjacent to the stomach/pancreatic region, causing mass effect on nearby structures. No obvious calcification or gas is visible within it on this image.

thought: No measurement labels are visible. The mass is in the left upper quadrant (left upper abdomen). Its size cannot be determined from the provided image description.
